name: InstancePath
summary: The InstancePath class represent the instance path to a given entity within the model hierarchy.

params.name: path
params.doc: The leaf can be any entity, but the rest must be a group or component instance.

throws.when: if the instance path isn't composed of instances and an optional leaf entity.

methods.summary: Note: This method does not accept negative indices.
methods.remarks: Note: This method does not accept negative indices. For the exact behavior of an array, use {#to_a}. The elements of an instance path can be accessed similarly to an array.

throws.when: if the given index is out of bounds
if the index is not of integer type
if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The yielded entities will start with the root and end with the leaf.
methods.remarks: The yielded entities will start with the root and end with the leaf.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.
if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: include?
methods.summary: Returns `true` if the instance path contain the given object.
methods.remarks: Returns `true` if the instance path contain the given object.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: leaf
methods.summary: The leaf of an instance path is the last element which can be any entity that can be represented in the model.
methods.remarks: The leaf of an instance path is the last element which can be any entity that can be represented in the model. This is normally a Drawingelement, but could be a Vertex. An instance can also be a leaf.

returns.doc: Nil if the last item of the instance path is a group or component, otherwise Entity.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.
if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: persistent_id_path
methods.summary: The serialized version of an instance path is the persistent ids of its entities concatenated with a period.
methods.remarks: The serialized version of an instance path is the persistent ids of its entities concatenated with a period.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: root
methods.summary: The root of an instance path is the element located closest to the model root.
methods.remarks: The root of an instance path is the element located closest to the model root. This will be a group or component instance. If you have a non-instance as a leaf with no other parent component this will return `nil`.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.
if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: Returns an array representing the instance path.
methods.remarks: Returns an array representing the instance path.

returns.doc: an array representing the instance path.

throws.when: if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

returns.doc: the combined transformation up to the the leaf entity.

throws.when: if the given index is out of bounds
if the index is not of integer type
if the instance path refer to deleted entities.

methods.name: valid?
methods.summary: An instance path is valid if it has at least one element and consist of groups and instances with exception of the leaf which can be any entity.
methods.remarks: An instance path is valid if it has at least one element and consist of groups and instances with exception of the leaf which can be any entity. This method doesn't check if the path can actually be looked up in the model.

properties.summary: Returns `true` if the instances paths represent the same set of entities.
properties.remarks: Returns `true` if the instances paths represent the same set of entities.
